commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3b51b0da2655b434dc2a8da1dceba8ffe97fb3c6)
commands.review_summary:
Adds a Discord inbound run watchdog that aborts queued message processing after a 30-minute default, commits replay keys on timeout, and extends queue regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +125. Total +189 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes from source: createChannelRunQueue awaits each keyed task with no per-task timeout, so a non-settling Discord processing promise can hold later jobs for the same key; I did not execute the new synthetic regression test.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Maintainers need to decide the Discord watchdog compatibility and same-session overlap contract before an automated repair can safely choose the final behavior.

target.title: fix(discord): unblock inbound queue after stuck run
target.body:
## Summary

This adds a Discord-specific watchdog around queued inbound message runs so one stuck run cannot indefinitely block later messages for the same Discord session key.

Default watchdog: 30 minutes.

## Problem

Discord inbound handling serializes work per route/session key. If one queued message run never settles after a stuck agent/tool path, later Discord messages for that same session key remain behind the unresolved queue tail. To users, later messages can look silently dropped because no new automatic agent turn starts.

Manual session injection can still work because it bypasses the Discord inbound run queue.

## Root cause

`createDiscordMessageRunQueue()` delegates to `createChannelRunQueue()`, which serializes per key and intentionally imposes no per-task timeout. A non-settling Discord inbound job can therefore hold the queue tail indefinitely.

## Fix

Wrap each Discord queued message run in a Discord-owned watchdog:

- pass a merged abort signal into `processDiscordMessage`;
- after 30 minutes, abort that signal;
- commit replay keys for the timed-out job;
- throw/log `DiscordMessageRunTimeoutError`;
- allow the keyed queue to advance to later messages.

Late completion of the original timed-out job is ignored so replay keys are not committed twice.

## Real behavior proof

Non-production proof, using the Discord monitor queue regression suite on current `origin/main` with this patch applied.

The new regression test exercises the real queue behavior at the Discord message-handler layer:

1. enqueue message `m-1` for a Discord session key;
2. make the first queued Discord run never resolve;
3. enqueue message `m-2` for the same session key;
4. advance the watchdog timeout;
5. verify the first run's abort signal is aborted;
6. verify the second same-session message is processed instead of staying stuck behind `m-1`;
7. redeliver `m-1` and verify replay protection prevents duplicate processing;
8. allow the original timed-out run to resolve late and verify it does not double-commit replay state or log a second failure.

This demonstrates the user-visible behavior this PR changes: a stuck Discord run no longer permanently blocks later same-session Discord inbound messages.

Validation command:

```text
openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.queue.test.ts
```

Result:

```text
1 file passed, 19 tests passed
```

I did not run this against a live production Gateway/runtime. The proof uses an isolated source checkout and synthetic Discord inbound jobs so it can exercise the stuck-queue behavior without mutating live Discord sessions or runtime state.

## Replay semantics

A watchdog timeout is treated like a non-retryable consumed inbound delivery. This favors avoiding duplicate processing over replaying a message whose processing state is unknown.

## Scope / relation to existing work

This is intentionally narrower than #79562. It does not add queue caps, drop policy, persistence, or global channel queue behavior changes. It only prevents a stuck Discord message run from blocking later same-session Discord inbound messages indefinitely.

Related: #9238, #75346, #29497 / #29532, #79562.

## Risks / limitations

The watchdog aborts cooperatively. If downstream work ignores abort signals, it may still complete later, so a timed-out run can overlap with a newer same-session run. This is preferred over an indefinite queue stall but is not a hard cancellation mechanism.

Full root `tsc -p tsconfig.json --noEmit` was attempted in an isolated checkout but hit a Node heap OOM before type diagnostics. The focused Discord queue regression suite and diff check passed on current `origin/main`.

## Tests

```text
git diff --check -- extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-run-queue.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.queue.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/timeouts.ts
```

```text
openclaw-heavy-run --backend auto -- pnpm exec vitest run extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.queue.test.ts
```

Targeted result:

```text
1 file passed, 19 tests passed
```
